channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US to impose new tariffs on countries over forced labor concerns
item.description: The US is set to introduce new tariffs targeting 60 countries over their alleged failure to combat forced labor, replacing the expiring 10% global levy imposed by Trump. Trade envoy Jamieson Greer con...

item.title: ‘Ohh My Dog’ trailer: Amit Rai’s film follows the search for a missing pet
item.description: The trailer of the Hindi film ‘Ohh My Dog’, directed by Amit Rai, has been released, offering a glimpse into a story centred around the search for a beloved missing canine. The film features a cast in...

item.title: India's Youth-Led 'Cockroach' Protests Continue After Police Clashes
item.description: India's youth-led 'Cockroach' protests, demanding educational reforms and government accountability, have escalated after violent clashes with police. The movement, which began as a satirical protest ...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs sign Tanzanian midfielder as potential Cele successor
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs have completed their third transfer window signing by securing 27-year-old Tanzanian midfielder Adolf Mtasingwa Bitegeko from Azam FC. This marks the first time a Tanzanian player has jo...

item.title: Former UK politician attacked and killed by man charged with murder
item.description: Former British politician Ann Widdecombe, 78, was murdered by Joshua Kerry, 28, who attacked her at her home in southwest England. Prosecutors revealed Kerry struck her 21 times with a hammer during l...

item.title: Chinese Envoy and Marcos Discuss Bilateral Ties and South China Sea Negotiations
item.description: Chinese Ambassador Jing Quan met with President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. to address tensions following a recent incident at Ayungin Shoal. The meeting, described as 'constructive,' focused on bilateral...

item.title: Petrol and diesel prices rise amid global price volatility and regional tensions
item.description: The government has raised petrol prices by Rs4.93 and high-speed diesel (HSD) by Rs7.15 per litre, effective July 22, to address fluctuating international market rates exacerbated by renewed hostiliti...

item.title: Freddy Brazier and Grandmother Attack by Bulldog: Hospitalization Follows Incident
item.description: Freddy Brazier, 21, was hospitalized after being attacked by his bulldog Pablo during a visit to his grandmother Jackiey Budden's London home. The incident occurred when Jackiey attempted to separate ...

item.title: Etsy Sellers Report Declining Sales as AI-Generated and Mass-Produced Listings Reshape the Marketplace
item.description: Many longtime Etsy sellers say the platform has changed significantly as AI-generated artwork, mass-produced products, and drop-shipped goods have become increasingly common. The article highlights th...

item.title: Website blocks bot traffic with error message
item.description: The article details a technical error message displayed by News Corp Australia's website, indicating that the user has been detected as a crawler bot. The message explains that the site uses software ...

item.title: McMichael Art Gallery to undergo three-year renovation and expansion
item.description: The McMichael Canadian Art Collection, located in Kleinburg, Ontario, will close its doors next week for a comprehensive three-year renovation and expansion project. The gallery, home to one of Canada...

item.title: Uganda's Virus Research Institute Launches as EAC Regional Virology Hub
item.description: The East African Community (EAC) is set to officially launch the Uganda Virus Research Institute (UVRI) as the EAC Regional Centre of Excellence in Virology on July 22, 2026. This milestone, celebrate...

item.title: Senator-Judges Argue Against Public Access to Sara Duterte's Financial Records
item.description: Five senator-judges have expressed opposition to the disclosure of Sara Duterte's tax and bank records, citing concerns over privacy rights and the legal process. The debate centers on whether such in...

item.title: Adrian Portelli's Partner Karlie Butler: A Private Life with Two Children
item.description: Adrian Portelli, an Australian billionaire entrepreneur, is in a long-term relationship with Karlie Butler, the mother of his two children, Mercy and Ayla. Karlie, from Bendigo, Victoria, maintains a ...

item.title: CBN retains interest rate at 26.5% as economic stability remains key
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has decided to keep the benchmark interest rate at 26.5% following a meeting of its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC). The governor, Mr Olayemi Cardoso, explained that ...

item.title: Falling migration: A political victory with economic challenges
item.description: The article discusses how falling migration, while politically advantageous for leaders, poses significant economic challenges. It highlights the UK's experience with net migration dropping to 171,000...

item.title: Andrea Johnson defends Idac's role in Khumalo corruption case
item.description: Adv Andrea Johnson, head of the Investigating Directorate Against Corruption (Idac), testified at the Madlanga commission of inquiry, countering allegations that her office targeted police crime intel...

item.title: SASSA reviews 350,000 social grants to combat fraud and ensure integrity
item.description: Social Development Minister Dina Pule has announced that over 350,000 social grants will be reviewed this financial year as part of SASSA's ongoing verification process. The initiative aims to safegua...

item.title: UK Drought Conditions Worsen as Parts of England Face Prolonged Dry Weather
item.description: Parts of southern, central, and eastern England are now close to drought conditions, according to the National Drought Group. Regions such as East Anglia, the West Midlands, Devon, Cornwall, and Londo...

item.title: Controversies Surround Pauline Hanson's Euro Trip and One Nation's Response
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing controversy surrounding Pauline Hanson's recent trip to Europe, which has generated significant negative headlines. Her comments during the trip have prompted members...
